title: rosa spinosissima subsp. pimpinellifolia
intro: Rosa spinosissima subsp. pimpinellifolia, also known as the Scotch rose or Burn rose, is a thorny shrub native to the northern parts of the United Kingdom. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Rosa spinosissima subsp. pimpinellifolia include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
